response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine geospatial exposure through GISCollective and Green Map, plus low-latency distributed systems experience from Dunnhumby, but the role's core requirement — modern C++ or Rust in a commercial or production context — is essentially absent from his profile. His brief C++ work at National Instruments dates to 2014–2015 and was tool/IDE-level, not systems or pipeline work. His entire recent career (2018–present) is built on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with no routing tile pipelines, navigation guidance systems, or geospatial data processing at production scale.
strengths: Geospatial domain familiarity via GISCollective platform and Green Map GIS application, including event-driven geo-data architecture
Low-latency distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and high-performance caching
Strong cross-stack adaptability and a track record of learning new domains (embedded, mobile, robotics, GIS)
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust in any commercial or recent production setting — C++ use is isolated to a 2014–2015 stint and a student thesis project
Primary stack throughout career is TypeScript/Node.js, which has no meaningful overlap with the role's systems-programming requirements
No experience with routing tile pipelines, navigation guidance systems, or geospatial data processing at the scale and reliability level the role demands
